data.title: Bitcoin Dominates as Altcoin Season Index Declines
data.description: CoinMarketCap's Altcoin Season Index has dropped by 4 points to 25, indicating a decrease in altcoin strength compared to Bitcoin. According to NS3.AI, the index measures whether the majority of the top 100 market cap coins have outperformed Bitcoin over the past 90 days. A score of 25 suggests that Bitcoin is currently leading the market in comparison to altcoins.

data.title: US stocks closed mixed in the crypto sector, with ALTS rising over 10%.
data.description: According to data from msx.com, at the close of trading on the US stock market, the Dow Jones Industrial Average fell 0.83%, the S&P 500 rose 0.41%, and the Nasdaq Composite rose 0.91%. The crypto sector was mixed, with ALTS rising over 10% and HUT rising over 9.66%; SBET rose over 6.5%, while HODL fell over 6.37%. msx.com is a decentralized RWA trading platform that has listed hundreds of RWA tokens, covering US stock and ETF tokens such as AAPL, AMZN, GOOGL, META, MSFT, NFLX, and NVDA.

data.title: SoftBank Plans Significant Investment in OpenAI Amid Major Funding Efforts
data.description: SoftBank Group is in discussions with artificial intelligence company OpenAI to potentially invest up to $30 billion. According to Foresight News, insiders have revealed that this move would significantly increase SoftBank's existing substantial stake in the ChatGPT developer. This investment is part of OpenAI's larger fundraising initiative, as the company aims to secure up to $100 billion from investors. If fully realized, this funding round could elevate OpenAI's valuation to approximately $830 billion.

data.title: U.S. Officials Investigate Maduro Regime's Funding Sources
data.description: U.S. officials are examining the financial sources of the Maduro regime, focusing on whether these assets involve physical commodities or digital assets. According to BlockBeats, Patrick Witt, Executive Director of the White House Digital Asset Advisory Committee, stated that several individuals within the national security system are actively participating in this investigation.

data.title: Trend Research borrowed $80 million USDT from Aave and transferred it to Binance.
data.description: According to on-chain analyst Ember, after ETH rebounded to $3,000, Trend Research borrowed $80 million USDT from Aave and transferred it to Binance in the last two hours.

data.title: Coinbase and Glassnode: Bitcoin is entering a more stable phase with significantly reduced leverage risk.
data.description: In their latest report, "Charting Crypto: Q1 2026," Coinbase's institutional research division and on-chain analytics firm Glassnode noted that Bitcoin is exhibiting more stable and resilient market characteristics. The report argues that the Q4 2025 correction has largely cleared excess leverage from the market, reducing Bitcoin's sensitivity to cascading liquidations and making it more resilient to macroeconomic shocks. The report states that the current market is not a return to a high-leverage speculative cycle, but rather a gradual shift towards a "macroeconomically sensitive asset," with its price more influenced by global liquidity, institutional positioning, and portfolio rebalancing. Unlike previous cycles dominated by retail momentum and high-leverage trading, the current market structure is more restrained, with institutional investors favoring defensive allocations. Researchers point out that the crypto market will be healthier overall entering 2026, with a relatively stable macroeconomic environment and supportive monetary policy expectations. The report also mentions that Coinbase's self-built global M2 money supply index historically leads Bitcoin prices by approximately 110 days, and this indicator remains positively correlated in the current quarter, suggesting short-term support for Bitcoin, although subsequent liquidity growth may slow. Furthermore, the report shows that open interest in Bitcoin options has surpassed that of perpetual contracts, indicating that investors are more inclined to purchase downside protection rather than continue leveraging to bet on directional market movements, reflecting a more cautious market risk appetite. (Decrypt)

Odaily reminds users that Meme coin prices are highly volatile, and investors should be aware of the risks.

data.title: A major ETH whale sold 10,000 stETH, making a profit of $1.039 million.
data.description: According to on-chain analyst Ai Yi, an address (0xd45...FB20) that previously profited $98.18 million from ETH trading has sold for the first time since establishing its position on January 12th. Three hours ago, this address deposited 10,000 stETH (worth $30.12 million) into Wintermute and subsequently received 30.03 million USDC. This address has recently accumulated 70,013 ETH at an average cost of approximately $2,907, and this sale resulted in a profit of $1.039 million. Currently, the address still holds 60,013 ETH with a floating profit of $7.243 million, bringing its total ETH trading profit to $99.22 million.

data.title: Significant Ethereum Transfer Observed Between Anonymous Addresses
data.description: A substantial transfer of Ethereum has been reported. According to ChainCatcher, at 07:57, a total of 3,310.07 ETH was moved from an anonymous address starting with 0xd8bC to another anonymous address beginning with 0xc17a. The transaction highlights ongoing activity within the cryptocurrency space, where large transfers between unidentified parties are not uncommon.

data.title: Arthur Hayes: If the Federal Reserve expands its balance sheet to stabilize the yen and Japanese government bonds, it will benefit risk assets such as Bitcoin.
data.description: In a recent article, BitMEX co-founder Arthur Hayes analyzed that the recent continuous depreciation of the yen and the decline in Japanese government bond prices are causing "unusual noises" in global financial markets. He believes that the Federal Reserve and the US Treasury may be forced to join forces to directly intervene in the yen exchange rate and the Japanese government bond market by expanding their balance sheets, thereby injecting new liquidity into the global fiat currency system. Hayes speculated that potential pathways might include: the New York Fed creating dollar reserves, instructing primary dealers such as JPMorgan Chase to sell dollars and buy yen in the foreign exchange market to support the exchange rate, and allocating some funds to Japanese government bonds to suppress yields. This move would increase the size of "foreign currency-denominated assets" on the Fed's balance sheet, essentially equivalent to absorbing the risks of the yen exchange rate and Japanese government bond interest rates through "money printing." He pointed out that the core objective of such operations is to stabilize the yen, curb the rise in Japanese government bond yields, prevent Japanese funds from massively selling US Treasury bonds and flowing back to Japan, thereby avoiding an uncontrolled rise in US Treasury yields and, to some extent, enhancing the competitiveness of US exports. At the same time, global dollar liquidity will increase accordingly, and the exchange rates of currencies such as the euro and the renminbi may also passively strengthen. Arthur Hayes emphasized that this "disguised quantitative easing"—not traditional QE—may ultimately be a medium- to long-term positive for risk assets, including Bitcoin. Regarding trading, he stated that a rapid strengthening of the yen is usually a signal of pressure on risk assets; therefore, he will not rashly increase his risk exposure before confirming that the Federal Reserve has officially intervened in the yen and Japanese government bond markets through quantitative easing. He revealed that he has temporarily closed positions in highly leveraged Bitcoin-related assets such as Strategy and Metaplanet; if his assessment is verified, he will re-enter the market. While awaiting policy clarity, his fund, Maelstrom, continues to increase its holdings of Zcash, while maintaining its positions in other high-quality DeFi tokens; once the Federal Reserve confirms quantitative easing intervention, he will consider increasing his holdings in DeFi assets such as ENA, ETHFI, Pendle, and LDO.

data.title: The "1011 Insider Whale" has increased its ETH long positions by 5 times, bringing its total holdings to over $640 million.
data.description: According to Onchain Lens monitoring, as the market rebounded, the "1011 insider whale" further increased its 5x ETH long position. Currently, this address's main holdings include: 212,726 ETH, worth approximately $643 million; 511,612.85 SOL, worth approximately $66 million; and 572.71 BTC, worth approximately $51.09 million. Despite the continued increase in holdings, the whale is still in a floating loss state, with current unrealized losses of approximately $29.79 million, and has already paid over $8 million in funding fees.

data.title: South Dakota lawmakers have introduced a new Bitcoin reserve bill, proposing to allow up to 10% of public funds to be invested in BTC.
data.description: South Dakota Representative Logan Manhart reintroduced the Bitcoin Reserve Bill HB 1155 in the state legislature on Tuesday. The bill is largely consistent with its 2025 version, which was shelved, with only minor revisions. The bill proposes amending state law to allow the South Dakota Investment Board to allocate up to 10% of its public funds to Bitcoin. Manhart posted on the X platform, "Strong money, strong state." If the bill passes the legislature and is signed by the governor, South Dakota will join the few U.S. states that allow state-level investment or holding of Bitcoin. Currently, only Texas, Arizona, and New Hampshire have passed similar legislation, and other states are proposing similar proposals. (Cointelegraph)

data.title: PayPal: Large enterprises are leading the adoption of encrypted payments, with nearly 85% of merchants expecting it to become the norm within 5 years.
data.description: A recent PayPal survey reveals that nearly 85% of surveyed merchants expect cryptocurrency payments to become commonplace within the next five years. The survey, conducted in late October 2025, covered approximately 620 "payment strategy decision-makers." The data shows that nearly 90% of merchants reported receiving inquiries from consumers about using cryptocurrency payments, with about 40% already supporting crypto payments at checkout. Among merchants accepting crypto payments, a significant portion indicated that crypto payments now account for more than a quarter of their total sales, and about three-quarters reported an increase in related sales over the past year. May Zabaneh, Vice President and General Manager of PayPal's Crypto Business, stated that crypto payments are moving from the experimental stage to everyday business applications, driven primarily by consumer demand for faster and more flexible payment methods. Once merchants begin adopting them, the real value will become apparent. In terms of scale, the adoption of crypto payments is primarily driven by large enterprises. Approximately 50% of companies with annual revenue exceeding $500 million already accept crypto payments, compared to 34% for small businesses and 32% for medium-sized businesses. Furthermore, approximately 90% of merchants indicated they would be willing to try encrypted payments if the experience and integration process were comparable to traditional bank card payments. This survey is closely related to the regulatory developments in stablecoins. Following the enactment of the GENIUS Act, the issuance and trading of stablecoins have received clear guidance. PayPal, as one of the earliest mainstream payment institutions to deploy stablecoins, is seeing its practices followed by more banks and fintech companies. (The Block)

data.title: Nomura Holdings subsidiary Laser Digital applies for a National Trust Banking License in the United States
data.description: Laser Digital, the digital asset arm of Japanese financial giant Nomura Holdings, has applied to the U.S. Office of the Comptroller of the Currency (OCC) for a national trust bank license, attempting to relocate its crypto and fintech operations entirely back to the United States amid a more relaxed regulatory environment in Washington. Sources have confirmed the application to The Block. If approved, a federal-level trust bank license would allow Laser Digital to operate nationwide without needing to apply for state-by-state custody licenses, but would not allow it to accept retail deposits. The report states that the company is also expected to offer spot cryptocurrency trading services. This move comes amid a new wave of crypto-native companies moving ashore. As U.S. policies become more inclusive, more institutions are seeking to integrate trading, custody, and settlement operations under a federal regulatory framework. The OCC typically grants national trust bank licenses with "conditional approval," requiring applicants to meet regulatory conditions regarding capital, governance, and compliance before commencing operations. Previously, World Liberty Financial, backed by Trump, stated it was seeking a national trust bank license to support the issuance and redemption of the USD1 stablecoin under a regulated entity. In addition, Circle, Ripple, BitGo, Fidelity Digital Assets, and Paxos have also received conditional approval from the OCC and are moving towards becoming federally regulated trust banks. (The Block)

data.title: CME Adjusts Margin Rates for Silver, Platinum, and Palladium Futures
data.description: On January 28, the Chicago Mercantile Exchange (CME) announced changes to the margin parameters for certain silver, platinum, and palladium futures contracts. According to BlockBeats, the notification was issued on January 27, local time. The document indicates that the new margin rates for some silver contracts will be higher than previous levels, approximately 11% of the nominal value. These new standards will take effect after the close of trading on January 28, local time. The announcement did not include any adjustments to gold-related contracts.

data.title: Binance launches a $200,000 ESPORTS trading competition; the token rises 15% in the same period.
data.description: Binance recently launched an ESPORTS trading competition with a total prize pool of $200,000. The leaderboard only tracks users' cumulative buy volume; sell transactions are not counted, thus encouraging participants to hold ESPORTS during the event period. While the trading competition is underway, Yooldo is accelerating the official launch of its first game, "Dungeon Log: Dagger Hit." This game is the first real-world application of the ESPORTS token after its issuance and recently completed its closed beta test, with 500 users participating and an average rating of 4.7 out of 5. According to the official plan, the game will be officially released in March. Compared to the test version, the official version will introduce a more complete growth system and richer gameplay mechanics, with ESPORTS and X402 being incorporated into the core design.

data.title: Russia Implements Network-Level Restrictions on Crypto Media Sites
data.description: Russian authorities have discreetly imposed network-level restrictions on several international cryptocurrency media websites. According to NS3.AI, these measures have been implemented without any official announcements. The restrictions utilize Deep Packet Inspection technology and are inconsistently applied across various internet service providers, impacting approximately one in four crypto news platforms accessed from domestic networks. Unlike traditional centralized shutdown methods, these restrictions are not listed in public blacklists.

data.title: Sol Meme coin Copperinus surged over 160 times in a single day, briefly pushing its market capitalization above $8 million.
data.description: According to GMGN data, Solana Chain's Meme coin, Copperinus, surged over 160 times within a day, briefly pushing its market capitalization above $8 million. It is currently trading at approximately $7.97 million. Odaily reminds users that Meme coin prices are highly volatile, and investors should be aware of the risks.

data.title: Morgan Stanley Enhances Digital Asset Strategy with Key Appointment
data.description: Morgan Stanley has named Amy Oldenburg to lead its digital asset strategy, marking a significant institutional interest in cryptocurrencies. According to NS3.AI, this strategic appointment coincides with the firm's initiatives to apply for Bitcoin and Solana ETFs and the introduction of a digital wallet. These actions indicate Morgan Stanley's readiness for anticipated shifts in digital asset legislation and the growing acceptance of stablecoins.

data.title: BNB Surpasses 900 USDT with a 2.44% Increase in 24 Hours
data.description: On Jan 27, 2026, 23:29 PM(UTC). According to Binance Market Data, BNB has crossed the 900 USDT benchmark and is now trading at 900.299988 USDT, with a narrowed 2.44% increase in 24 hours.
